name: Classic Rock
description: Rock legends, bands, and anthems from the 60s–80s

questions.q: In which country was the rock band AC/DC formed in 1973?

source.notes: Formed in Sydney by Malcolm and Angus Young.

questions.q: Mick Jagger is the lead singer of which classic rock band?

source.notes: Band formed 1962; Jagger is an original member.

questions.q: Which guitarist wrote the song 'Layla' about his love for George Harrison's wife, Pattie Boyd?

source.notes: Released 1970 with Derek and the Dominos; Clapton later married Pattie Boyd.

questions.q: Which Boston-based rock band recorded the songs 'Dream On' and 'Walk This Way'?

source.notes: Steven Tyler on vocals, Joe Perry on lead guitar.

questions.q: Keith Moon, famous for his wild drumming, was the drummer of which British rock band?

source.notes: Drummed for The Who from 1964 until his death in 1978.

questions.q: Jim Morrison was the lead singer of which Los Angeles rock band?

source.notes: Formed 1965 in Los Angeles; named for Aldous Huxley's 'The Doors of Perception'.

questions.q: Which British band recorded 'Bohemian Rhapsody' and 'Another One Bites the Dust'?

source.notes: 'Bohemian Rhapsody' (1975) held #1 in the UK for 9 weeks.

questions.q: In which year was Led Zeppelin formed?

source.notes: Formed in London by Jimmy Page after the breakup of The Yardbirds.

questions.q: Brian May is the guitarist and co-songwriter of which legendary British band?

source.notes: Has a PhD in astrophysics; co-founded Queen in 1970.

questions.q: What is the title of Pink Floyd's 1973 concept album, one of the best-selling of all time?
choices: The Dark Side of the Moon

source.notes: Spent 741 weeks on the Billboard 200 chart.

questions.q: Who replaced Ozzy Osbourne as the vocalist of Black Sabbath in 1979?

source.notes: Dio fronted Sabbath from 1979–1982 and 1991–1992.

questions.q: Who was the original drummer of Led Zeppelin whose death in 1980 ended the band?

source.notes: Bonham died September 25, 1980; Led Zeppelin disbanded shortly after.

questions.q: Which New Jersey-raised singer-songwriter is known as 'The Boss'?

source.notes: Earned the nickname early on for collecting and divvying up the band's pay.

questions.q: Before his successful solo career, Phil Collins was the drummer and later lead singer of which band?

source.notes: Took over lead vocals after Peter Gabriel left in 1975.

questions.q: Which California rock band recorded the 1976 album 'Hotel California'?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: album released Dec 8, 1976 (title-track single Feb 1977). Sold over 32 million copies worldwide.

questions.q: 'Purple Haze' was a 1967 hit for which guitarist's group?

source.notes: Featured on their debut album 'Are You Experienced'.

questions.q: Ziggy Stardust was the glam-rock alter-ego of which British artist?

source.notes: Introduced on the 1972 album 'The Rise and Fall of Ziggy Stardust and the Spiders from Mars'.

questions.q: Before they became Led Zeppelin, the band briefly performed as which outfit?

source.notes: Jimmy Page formed the New Yardbirds in 1968 to fulfil Yardbirds tour commitments.

questions.q: In which year did Black Sabbath release the album 'Paranoid'?

source.notes: Second Sabbath LP; includes 'Iron Man' and 'War Pigs'.

questions.q: Pink Floyd were briefly known by which earlier name before settling on Pink Floyd in 1965?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: replaced 'The Tea Set' (also a real prior name) with 'The Hermits' to leave a single correct answer. Full name 'The Screaming Abdabs'.

questions.q: Progressive rock trio Rush hail from which country?

source.notes: Formed in Toronto in 1968; known for tracks like 'Tom Sawyer' and 'Limelight'.

questions.q: Jim Morrison of The Doors died in Paris in 1971 at what age — making him a member of the '27 Club'?

source.notes: Died July 3, 1971. Also in the '27 Club': Brian Jones, Jimi Hendrix, Janis Joplin, Kurt Cobain, Amy Winehouse.
